designer: Gardener & Son (planned — design phase commencing within 1-3 months)
description: A 60m² raised-bed garden on the northern face of Canterbury Road, Surrey Hills — 11 species across 142 plants, two strictly local-indigenous (Hardenbergia violacea and Lomandra longifolia) among a mixed palette maintained by G&S via Tend. Surrey Park bounds the southern edge. G&S design intervention commissioned — this baseline captures the pre-intervention state; the trajectory from 16 Jun 2026 will be the registry's first independently-legible before/after evidence for a Tend-maintained property.

biodiversity.biodiversity_notes: Of 11 species, only Hardenbergia violacea and Lomandra longifolia are strictly local-indigenous to the Surrey Hills Grassy Woodland EVC. A further 4 species are Australian-native but not local (Syzygium smithii, Syzygium australe, Hymenosporum flavum, Banksia ericifolia) — these contribute to fauna habitat but not local ecological function. The remaining 5 species are exotic ornamentals (Dichondra argentea, Anemone hupehensis, Camellia sp., Ficus pumila, Trachelospermum jasminoides).

soil_water.soil_notes: Imported soil in raised beds. Mulch applied by G&S maintenance team. Drainage is poor — beds retain water and have been observed soggy; future design intervention should address subsurface drainage or convert to in-ground planting on the natural soil profile.

habitat.habitat_notes: No habitat features currently present. Future design intervention to introduce logs, rocks, and consider nest box / water feature.

connectivity.park_description: Surrey Park, large public park on Canterbury Road, with mature trees and remnant vegetation. Functions as connecting habitat for the garden.

milestones.title: Upgrade to G&S Verified after design intervention

activity_log.title: Baseline assessment — pre-intervention registration
activity_log.notes: G&S site assessment of pre-existing garden maintained via Tend. 11 species, 142 plants in 60m² raised-bed area. 2 strictly local-indigenous species (Hardenbergia violacea, Lomandra longifolia). Surrey Park adjacent south of Canterbury Road. G&S design intervention commissioned — baseline captured 16 Jun 2026 for before/after trajectory.

notes: Pre-intervention baseline, 16 Jun 2026. Garden was not designed or installed by G&S — G&S has maintained via Tend service. Design intervention commissioned, expected within 1-3 months. verification_level will move to gardener_and_son_verified after design intervention is installed. Garden coordinates from Google Maps long-press (6dp precision). Surrey Park coordinates (park_lat -37.8265, park_lng 145.096) are approximate — confirm at next site visit. Ward TBC — confirm with Boroondara council.

yield.status: Not yet active — yield layer in roadmap
yield.formula_note: Score x area x connectivity x verification. Formula v1.

upgrades.action: Increase indigenous species to 12+ via G&S design intervention
Add habitat logs and rock refuges
